tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Drop shot works deep AND shallow — I fish it on 6-8ft flats just as effectively. Use a long cylindrical weight that pulls through vegetation and rocks. Tungsten for clean bottoms. Nose-hook a straight-tailed worm for unbelievable action. Use heavy enough weight to maintain consistent bottom contact — if 1/4oz isn't cutting it, go to 3/8oz.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Drop shot rod: fast-tapering tip with backbone for long casts. Most bend in the first third for sensitivity without sacrificing control. On the bite, don't jerk the rod — just reel in and let the tip load. The sharp hook takes care of the rest. Keep finger on the line to detect strikes.'
